message:
feat(layout,render-svg,render-term): refine sequence note geometry and enhance SVG/terminal rendering

- Improve sequence note geometry building with edge case handling
- Enhance SVG rendering with improved shape and path logic (+339 lines)
- Add cluster border color support in theme configuration
- Refactor terminal renderer UI rendering (~106 lines)
- Regenerate pie chart golden SVG and layout checksums
- Update beads issue tracking

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-3uz.9","title":"Add structured pressure/degradation diagnostics schema across CLI and WASM","description":"Define and emit structured pressure/degradation diagnostics so automated systems can reason about runtime behavior under stress.

Scope:
- Extend diagnostics schema with budget usage, pressure tier, degradation operators, and confidence.
- Emit in `fm-cli validate --format json` and trace bundle outputs.
- Expose equivalent data in WASM APIs for browser telemetry and debuggability.
- Add schema versioning and backward-compatible evolution rules.

Why this matters:
Graceful degradation is only operationally useful if it is machine-observable.

Acceptance criteria:
- Schema is documented and versioned.
- CLI and WASM emit consistent pressure diagnostics.
- Trace replay reproduces observed degradation decisions.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:57:34.661368152Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T04:28:01.844568701Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["diagnostics","schema","telemetry"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3uz.9","depends_on_id":"bd-2b4.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:57:36.931674026Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3uz.9","depends_on_id":"bd-3uz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:57:34.661368152Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3uz.9","depends_on_id":"bd-3uz.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:57:36.839676013Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":88,"issue_id":"bd-3uz.9","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Verification contract (mandatory): this bead must ship comprehensive unit tests, integration coverage, and E2E script coverage with detailed structured logging. Required logs include input metadata, algorithm or mode selection, diagnostics, degradation or fallback decisions where applicable, timing metrics, output hashes or sizes, and explicit pass fail reasons. CI artifacts must be sufficient for post-mortem debugging without rerun.","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:28:01Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-4gmf","title":"Requirement: Implement risk levels, verification methods, and traceability","description":"# Requirement Diagram Improvements

## Gaps
- Risk levels (high/medium/low) parsed but not visually indicated (color coding)
- Verification methods (analysis/inspection/test/demonstration) not rendered
- Traceability matrix view not available
- Element docRef not rendered as links
- Relationship type labels not visually differentiated

## Implementation Plan
1. Apply color coding to requirement boxes based on risk level (red=high, yellow=medium, green=low)
2. Show verification method as an icon or text badge on requirement nodes
3. Render element docRef as clickable link (same as flowchart click directive)
4. Differentiate relationship types visually (satisfies=solid, contains=dashed, etc.)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:47:12.232116666Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-17T05:42:24.479032580Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["parity","parser","render","requirement"],"comments":[{"id":170,"issue_id":"bd-4gmf","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Testing Contract (Mandatory)

This bead MUST ship with:

### Unit Tests
- Focused tests for every new parser path (positive + negative cases)
- Render output assertions (SVG element presence, attributes, structure)
- Edge cases: empty input, malformed input, boundary values

### Integration Tests
- Full pipeline: parse -> layout -> render for representative inputs
- Determinism check: same input -> identical output (3 runs)
- Graceful degradation: partially valid input produces best-effort output with diagnostics

### E2E Validation Script
Run via fm-cli with structured JSON logging. Required log fields: input_hash, diagram_type, node_count, edge_count, diagnostic_count, output_size_bytes, render_ms, pass_fail_reason.

### Quality Gates
All must pass before this bead can be closed:
- cargo check --workspace --all-targets
- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings  
- cargo fmt --check
- cargo test --workspace (zero new failures)
","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:30:44Z"},{"id":190,"issue_id":"bd-4gmf","author":"ubuntu","text":"SPECIFIC TEST CASES:
1. Requirement with risk:high → verify red color coding on requirement box
2. Requirement with verifymethod:test → verify badge/icon showing verification method
3. Element with docRef:'http://...' → verify clickable link
4. Relationship 'satisfies' → verify solid arrow line
5. Relationship 'contains' → verify dashed line or different style
6. Requirement with all properties (id, text, risk, verifymethod) → verify all render","created_at":"2026-03-17T05:42:24Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-508m","title":"Implement sequence diagram autonumber","description":"# Sequence Autonumber

## What mermaid-js Supports
\`\`\`
sequenceDiagram
  autonumber
  Alice->>Bob: First (1)
  Bob->>Alice: Second (2)
\`\`\`
- \`autonumber\` enables sequential numbering on all messages
- Numbers appear as a small circled number next to each message arrow
- Can be toggled on/off mid-diagram

## Current State
- NOT parsed. \`autonumber\` line produces a warning.

## Implementation Plan
1. **Parser**: Recognize \`autonumber\` as SequenceStatement::Autonumber.
2. **IR**: Add autonumber: bool flag to IrSequenceMeta.
3. **SVG Renderer**: When autonumber is true, prepend a circled number to each message label.
4. **Tests**: Autonumber on, verify numbers appear in SVG.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:44:09.887912141Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-18T02:02:25.665996068Z","closed_at":"2026-03-18T02:02:25.665460955Z","close_reason":"Implemented autonumber parsing and SVG rendering. Parser recognizes 'autonumber' keyword as SequenceStatement::Autonumber, sets IrSequenceMeta.autonumber=true via IrBuilder.enable_autonumber(). SVG renderer prepends edge number to labels when autonumber is enabled. 3 parser tests added. All quality gates pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["parity","parser","render","sequence"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-508m","depends_on_id":"bd-6zd9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:48:26.628593830Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":147,"issue_id":"bd-508m","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Testing Contract (Mandatory)

 {"id":"bd-6qd","title":"EPIC: Documentation, Packaging & Distribution","description":"Create comprehensive documentation, NPM packaging, crates.io publishing preparation, and distribution artifacts. Includes: README with examples, API documentation (rustdoc), mermaid syntax reference, migration guide from mermaid-js, NPM package for WASM module, GitHub releases with prebuilt binaries for CLI, and a documentation website.

## Success Criteria

- Every capability listed in this epic is delivered completely through child issues; no scope cuts and no loss of planned functionality.
- Child tasks include comprehensive unit/integration coverage and e2e coverage with detailed logging; test evidence is available and reproducible.
- Performance, determinism, and reliability expectations for this epic are validated with measurable checks.
- User-facing behavior is documented (README/docs/config/migration notes) for all newly shipped functionality.
- Release readiness is proven by passing workspace quality gates and epic-level validation flows end-to-end.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-11T16:37:05.020817581Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T01:46:27.650966183Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docs","epic"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-6qd","depends_on_id":"bd-2b4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T08:37:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-6qd","depends_on_id":"bd-2hq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T08:37:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-6qd","depends_on_id":"bd-2u0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T08:37:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-6qd","depends_on_id":"bd-2xl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T08:37:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":39,"issue_id":"bd-6qd","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Idea-wizard expansion: added bd-6qd.4 (versioned config schema + generated docs/types) and bd-6qd.5 (release canary matrix for CLI+WASM) for safer integrations and releases.","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:46:27Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-6qd.1","title":"Write README with feature comparison and examples","description":"Write README.md with: project description and motivation, feature comparison table vs mermaid-js (highlighting our advantages: cycle handling, error recovery, visual quality, WASM performance, Rust safety), installation instructions (CLI, NPM, cargo), quick-start examples for each diagram type, configuration reference, theme gallery screenshots, contribution guide. Include SVG example renders embedded in README.

## Acceptance Criteria

- All functionality explicitly described above is implemented fully; no scope reduction, no feature deletions, and no silent behavioral regressions.
- Code is modular, documented where needed, and integrated with existing crates/interfaces without compatibility shims or duplicated logic.
- Comprehensive automated tests are added/updated for this issue: focused unit tests + relevant integration coverage for touched behavior and edge cases.
- End-to-end validation is covered where applicable (CLI and/or WASM path) with detailed structured logging (inputs, detection results, node/edge counts, output sizes, timings, and pass/fail reasons).
- Diagnostics are actionable: failures/warnings include precise context and suggested remediation.
- Determinism and stability expectations are verified for affected outputs (layout/render/serialization as applicable).
- Quality gates pass: `cargo fmt --check`, `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`, `cargo check --all-targets`, and relevant `cargo test` scope.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-11T16:37:19.826112755Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T02:57:39.136967863Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docs"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-6qd.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2xl.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T08:37:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-6qd.1","depends_on_id":"bd-3uz.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:57:39.136915805Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-6qd.1","depends_on_id":"bd-6qd","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T08:37:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
@@ -330,6 +330,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-drav","title":"Parse and render sequence diagram interaction fragments (loop/alt/opt/par/critical/break)","description":"# Sequence Interaction Fragments

## What mermaid-js Supports
\`\`\`
sequenceDiagram
  Alice->>Bob: Request
  loop Every minute
    Bob->>Alice: Heartbeat
  end
  alt Success
    Bob->>Alice: 200 OK
  else Failure
    Bob->>Alice: 500 Error
  end
  opt Optional
    Alice->>Carol: Forward
  end
  par Parallel
    Alice->>Bob: Task 1
  and
    Alice->>Carol: Task 2
  end
  critical Critical Section
    Alice->>Bob: Important
  option Timeout
    Alice->>Bob: Retry
  end
  break When error
    Alice->>Bob: Abort
  end
  rect rgb(200, 220, 240)
    Alice->>Bob: Highlighted
  end
\`\`\`

Fragment types: loop, alt/else, opt, par/and, critical/option, break, rect (colored background).
Each fragment is a labeled box spanning the involved participants, enclosing the messages within.
Fragments can be nested.

## Current State
- NOT parsed. Fragment keywords produce warnings.
- No layout support for fragment regions.
- No SVG rendering of fragment boxes.

## Implementation Plan
1. **Parser**: Add SequenceStatement::FragmentStart { kind, label }, FragmentElse { label }, FragmentEnd. Track nesting depth. Kind enum: Loop, Alt, Opt, Par, Critical, Break, Rect.
2. **IR**: Store as Vec<IrSequenceFragment> with kind, label, start_message_index, end_message_index, participant_range, and nested children.
3. **Layout**: Fragments add padding around their contained messages. Compute fragment box bounds from the first/last message y-positions and the leftmost/rightmost participant x-positions. Nested fragments get progressively larger bounds.
4. **SVG Renderer**: Draw fragment boxes as dashed-border rectangles with a label tab in the top-left corner. Fragment kind shown in bold in the tab. 'else'/'and'/'option' dividers shown as dashed horizontal lines within the fragment.
5. **Tests**: loop, alt/else, opt, par/and, nested fragments, rect with color.

## Complexity
This is the single most complex sequence diagram feature. Fragment nesting requires a stack-based parser and recursive layout. Budget 2-3x the effort of a simple task.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:44:09.719718813Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-18T02:25:34.360084926Z","closed_at":"2026-03-18T02:25:34.359593546Z","close_reason":"Implemented sequence interaction fragment parsing (loop, alt, opt, par, critical, break, rect). Stack-based parser tracks nesting depth. Fragments store kind, label, start/end edge indices, alternatives (for else/and/option dividers), and nested child indices. 'end' keyword dispatches to close either fragments or box groups. 6 tests added covering all fragment types, alt/else alternatives, nested fragments, rect with color, and warning-free parsing.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["layout","parity","parser","render","sequence"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-drav","depends_on_id":"bd-6zd9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:48:25.838352205Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-drav","depends_on_id":"bd-7g6d","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:48:29.797041627Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":146,"issue_id":"bd-drav","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Testing Contract (Mandatory)

This bead MUST ship with:

### Unit Tests
- Focused tests for every new parser path (positive + negative cases)
- Render output assertions (SVG element presence, attributes, structure)
- Edge cases: empty input, malformed input, boundary values

### Integration Tests
- Full pipeline: parse -> layout -> render for representative inputs
- Determinism check: same input -> identical output (3 runs)
- Graceful degradation: partially valid input produces best-effort output with diagnostics

### E2E Validation Script
Run via fm-cli with structured JSON logging. Required log fields: input_hash, diagram_type, node_count, edge_count, diagnostic_count, output_size_bytes, render_ms, pass_fail_reason.

### Quality Gates
All must pass before this bead can be closed:
- cargo check --workspace --all-targets
- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings  
- cargo fmt --check
- cargo test --workspace (zero new failures)
","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:30:40Z"},{"id":175,"issue_id":"bd-drav","author":"ubuntu","text":"SCOPE NOTE: This bead is intentionally large because the fragment parser, layout integration, and renderer are tightly coupled. Splitting parser from renderer would create a half-baked intermediate state (parsed but invisible fragments). Implementors should budget 2-3x normal effort. Suggested approach: implement loop first (simplest fragment), then alt/else (adds alternation), then the rest. Each fragment type can be tested incrementally.

DETAILED TEST CASES:
1. Simple loop: loop Every minute / Bob->>Alice: ping / end → verify dashed box with 'loop' tab, contains 1 message
2. Alt with else: alt OK / A->>B: 200 / else Error / A->>B: 500 / end → verify two sections separated by dashed line
3. Nested: loop / alt / A->>B: x / else / A->>B: y / end / end → verify nested boxes with correct containment
4. Par with and: par / A->>B: t1 / and / A->>C: t2 / end → verify parallel sections
5. Rect with color: rect rgb(200,200,240) / A->>B: x / end → verify colored background
6. Empty fragment: loop Empty / end → verify empty box renders without crash
7. Fragment spanning all participants: verify box width covers leftmost to rightmost participant involved
8. 3-level nesting: loop / critical / opt / A->>B / end / end / end → verify 3 nested boxes","created_at":"2026-03-17T05:41:30Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-dsjl","title":"Sequence: implement rect background highlighting parity","description":"# Sequence rect highlighting parity

## Goal
Make Mermaid sequence `rect COLOR ... end` fragments render as actual background highlighting instead of a generic fragment box.

## Scope
- Preserve the rect color distinctly in sequence IR and layout extensions.
- Render SVG rect fragments with the configured fill color and a sensible border, while keeping unsafe paint values out of the output.
- Keep non-rect fragments on existing generic fragment rendering.
- Add focused parser/layout/render tests.

## Acceptance Criteria
- `rect rgb(...)` and `rect rgba(...)` render as highlighted sequence regions in SVG.
- `rect transparent` renders without an opaque fill.
- Existing loop/alt/opt/par/critical/break fragment rendering remains intact.
- Required cargo checks pass via `rch` and formatting is clean.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-23T21:15:29.112583706Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-23T21:19:40.040611440Z","closed_at":"2026-03-23T21:19:40.040101485Z","close_reason":"Implemented sequence rect background highlighting parity","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-e1sg","title":"Implement sequence participant aliases and box grouping","description":"# Sequence Participant Aliases and Box Grouping

## What mermaid-js Supports
\`\`\`
sequenceDiagram
  participant A as Alice
  actor B as Bob
  box Blue Team
    participant A
    participant B
  end
  A->>B: Hello
\`\`\`
- Participant aliases: \`participant shortName as Display Name\`
- Box grouping: groups participants visually in a colored box with label
- create/destroy: \`create participant C\` and \`destroy C\`

## Current State
- Alias syntax (\`as\`) IS parsed for participant declarations
- Box grouping is NOT parsed
- create/destroy are NOT parsed

## Implementation Plan
1. **Parser**: Parse \`box [color] Label ... end\` blocks. Parse \`create participant\` and \`destroy Name\`.
2. **IR**: Add participant groups and lifecycle events to IrSequenceMeta.
3. **Layout**: Group boxes add horizontal containment around grouped participants. Destroyed participants have shortened lifelines.
4. **SVG Renderer**: Draw participant group boxes. Draw destruction X on lifeline.
5. **Tests**: Alias rendering, box grouping, create/destroy lifecycle.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:44:10.055763292Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-18T02:16:15.741638433Z","closed_at":"2026-03-18T02:16:15.741110364Z","close_reason":"Implemented sequence participant box grouping and lifecycle events. Participant groups tracked in IrBuilder with auto-close on diagram end. 5 tests added covering box grouping with/without color, create/destroy events, and warning-free parsing.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["parity","parser","render","sequence"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-e1sg","depends_on_id":"bd-6zd9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:48:27.407572780Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":148,"issue_id":"bd-e1sg","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Testing Contract (Mandatory)

This bead MUST ship with:

### Unit Tests
- Focused tests for every new parser path (positive + negative cases)
- Render output assertions (SVG element presence, attributes, structure)
- Edge cases: empty input, malformed input, boundary values

### Integration Tests
- Full pipeline: parse -> layout -> render for representative inputs
- Determinism check: same input -> identical output (3 runs)
- Graceful degradation: partially valid input produces best-effort output with diagnostics

### E2E Validation Script
Run via fm-cli with structured JSON logging. Required log fields: input_hash, diagram_type, node_count, edge_count, diagnostic_count, output_size_bytes, render_ms, pass_fail_reason.

### Quality Gates
All must pass before this bead can be closed:
- cargo check --workspace --all-targets
- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings  
- cargo fmt --check
- cargo test --workspace (zero new failures)
","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:30:40Z"},{"id":202,"issue_id":"bd-e1sg","author":"ubuntu","text":"ADDITIONAL: mermaid-js also supports a 'links' keyword for attaching URLs to participants:
```
sequenceDiagram
  participant A as Alice
  links A: {"Dashboard": "https://...", "Wiki": "https://..."}
```
This renders as small link icons or a dropdown on the participant header. Parser should recognize 'links ParticipantName: {JSON}' and store URLs on the participant node.","created_at":"2026-03-17T06:27:08Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-f4er","title":"Block-Beta: Implement block styling and flexible column widths","description":"# Block-Beta Rendering Improvements

## Gaps
- Block styling (fill, stroke) not applied from classDef/style directives
- Column widths are fixed; should auto-size to content
- Arrow/connector visual styles not differentiated
- Nested group styling

## Implementation Plan
1. Apply style directives to block SVG elements
2. Compute column widths from maximum content width in each column
3. Support different arrow styles on block-beta connectors
4.  {"id":"bd-f6ow","title":"GitGraph: Implement commit type rendering and branch color coding","description":"# GitGraph Rendering Improvements

## Gaps
- Commit type styling: NORMAL (default), REVERSE (highlighted), HIGHLIGHT (emphasized) — types are parsed but all render the same
- Branch color coding: each branch should have a distinct color
- Tag rendering: tags are parsed but not visually rendered as labels on commits
- Cherry-pick visualization: should show dashed line from source to destination

## Implementation Plan
1. Apply distinct fill colors based on commit type
2. Assign branch colors from a palette (branch index → color)
3. Render tags as small rounded labels attached to commit nodes
4. Draw cherry-pick connections as dashed curved lines","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:46:07.978428547Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-17T05:41:31.232694211Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["gitgraph","parity","render"],"comments":[{"id":159,"issue_id":"bd-f6ow","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Testing Contract (Mandatory)

","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:30:42Z"},{"id":178,"issue_id":"bd-f6ow","author":"ubuntu","text":"SPECIFIC TEST CASES:
1. Commit with type:REVERSE → verify distinct fill color (e.g., highlighted/inverted)
2. Commit with type:HIGHLIGHT → verify emphasized styling
3. Three branches (main, develop, feature) → verify each gets a distinct color
4. Commit with tag:"v1.0" → verify tag label renders as small rounded badge on commit node
5. Cherry-pick from feature to main → verify dashed arc connecting the two commits
6. Branch with 10+ commits → verify no overlap, consistent spacing
7. Merge commit → verify it shows connections from both parent branches","created_at":"2026-03-17T05:41:31Z"}]}
